name: Sourdough Italian Bread
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Plus 1 teaspoon grated Romano or Parmesan cheese
recipeInstructions:
1. Early in the morning, combine the starter, 1 teaspoon salt, 1 cup lukewarm water, and 1-1/2 cups flour. Beat thoroughly until blended.
2. Cover with a clean towel and set aside for approximately 5 hours. If the sponge needs to set longer or shorter, it won't be harmed.
3. When ready to finish the dough, stir the sponge down.
4. Dissolve the yeast in the lukewarm water and add to the sponge.
5. Stir in the remaining ingredients (4 cups flour, salt, black pepper, Romano/Parmesan cheese) in the order given, adding 4 cups flour first, using the other cup of flour if needed.
6. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured breadboard and knead thoroughly for about 7 to 10 minutes.
7. Return the dough to a buttered bowl, cover with a clean towel, and set aside to rise until doubled (about 1 hour).
8. When doubled, punch down the dough and set aside, covered, for another 1/2 hour.
9. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured board again, divide into 6 pieces of dough.
10. Roll each piece into a long rope. Braid 3 ropes together for each loaf. Tuck the ends under and lift each braid into a buttered glass loaf pan.
11. Cover and set aside to rise until doubled again (about 1 hour).
12. Brush loaves with melted butter and sprinkle each loaf with 1 teaspoon sesame seed and grated Romano/Parmesan cheese.
13. Bake loaves in a 375°F oven until browned and done, about 40 to 45 minutes.
